name: Filo Mushroom Pies
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the onion finely and soften it in a little butter or oil. Fry until frazzled. Remove and reserve.
2. Dice the mushrooms and fry them in the remaining fat from the pan. Stir and turn as necessary to drive off most of their moisture and concentrate flavor. Mix with the cooked vegetables, season with salt and pepper, add cumin, coriander, and grated cheese.
3. Unwrap one sheet of Filo pastry at a time. Keep the rest covered with a damp cloth to prevent drying out.
4. Cut each sheet into strips about 3 x 10 inches long. Brush one side only with melted butter.
5. Place a rounded metal teaspoonful of the filling mixture near the short edge of the buttery side, about 1 inch from the bottom and slightly to the left.
6. Fold the bottom right corner diagonally over the filling to form a triangle. Continue folding the pastry at right angles up the entire length of the strip, resulting in a neat triangular parcel.
7. Repeat until all the filling is used.
8. Brush the pies with melted butter or oil, arrange them on baking trays, and bake at 350-375°F (180-190°C) gas mark 4/5 for about 20 minutes, until golden and crisp. Turn the pies over after the first 10 minutes and brush with more butter or oil.
9. For even crisper results, deep-fry the pies.
